text.title: Wave parallelism, worktree isolation, merge serialization
text.summary: The orchestrator groups features into execution waves based on file overlap, runs each wave in isolated `git worktree` checkouts, and serializes merges back into the default branch with `flock` so concurrent agents never corrupt the index.

section.title: Wave admission rules
section.p: Two features may run in the same wave if any of the following holds:

list.item: They live in different repositories (e.g. one in `faion-network/`, one in `faion-net-fe/`).
They live in the same repository but touch disjoint files.
They live in the same repository and same file, but at non-overlapping line ranges, AND the orchestrator accepts a possible rebase between them.

section.p: Otherwise the features go in sequential waves. When in doubt, default to a separate worktree per feature.

Pool size is bounded by quota: read `platform quota-state source`'s `rate_limits.five_hour.used_percentage`. Stop adding agents to the wave when the projected wave consumption would push the meter past the user-set threshold (default 50%).

rule.statement: Every parallel executor subagent works inside its own `git worktree add <isolated path> -b <feature branch> <default branch>` and removes the worktree after merging back.
rule.rationale: The main working tree often carries uncommitted local-only state (preview data, lock files like `scheduled_tasks.lock`, dev toggles). Concurrent agents touching it would corrupt each other. Worktrees give each agent a clean, branch-scoped checkout sharing the same `.git/` for cheap branching.

example.code:
REPO=/home/nero/workspace/projects/faion-net/faion-network FEATURE=F-073-go-error-handling WT=/tmp/wt-${FEATURE}
git -C "$REPO" worktree add "$WT" -b feature/${FEATURE} main cd "$WT" # ... edit, run validators, commit on feature branch ... flock /tmp/faion-network-merge.lock bash -c ' cd "'$REPO'" \ && git fetch origin main \ && git rebase origin/main feature/'$FEATURE' \ && git checkout main \ && git merge --ff-only feature/'$FEATURE' ' git -C "$REPO" worktree remove "$WT" git -C "$REPO" branch -d feature/${FEATURE}

rule.statement: Every executor subagent MUST verify branch state before any edit: run `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`, expect the canonical branch (main/master). Halt and report if the tree is detached or on an unexpected ref.
rule.rationale: Pool parallelism with `git worktree add` can leave the original tree in detached HEAD if a sibling agent does `git checkout` for any reason. Committing on detached HEAD silently loses work when the worktree closes. Observed in F-050 T09 (2026-05-10): faion-net-fe working tree found at pre-F-050 commit; recovered via `git checkout main`. No work lost only because the executor detected drift before committing.

rule.statement: The merge step MUST be wrapped in `flock /tmp/faion-network-merge.lock` (or the playbook-specific lock file). A subagent that cannot acquire the lock waits — it does not skip the merge and it does not race on `git checkout main`.
rule.rationale: Even with worktrees, the merge target (`main` / `master`) is a single ref. Two concurrent fast-forward merges race on `.git/HEAD`; one silently loses. `flock` makes the critical section observable and bounded.

rule.statement: An executor that hits a merge or rebase conflict MUST stop, list conflict files plus both sides' SDD intent (read each side's `spec.md` / `design.md`), and return control to the orchestrator. The resolver subagent (`prompts/06-merge-resolver.md`) is then dispatched.
rule.rationale: Auto-resolving with `--ours` / `--theirs` silently destroys one side. Conflict resolution is its own phase with its own prompt file, not a side effect of execution.

list.item: Reads both features' `spec.md` and `design.md` to understand the current and other side's intent.
Reads `git log` and `git show` for the conflicting file to confirm the diff.
Combines both intents at the line level, with a documented decision per file in the resolver's report.
Special-cases append-only files (`CHANGELOG.md`, `MAP.md`, `methodologies-index/methodology.xml`) by keeping entries from both sides under `## [Unreleased]` (or the equivalent heading).
